description: A topic is about to be studied for the first time and needs its initial 5-question verification set — the Skill must fire and produce exactly 5 source-anchored questions.

input.topic_source_material: Git is a distributed version control system that tracks changes in source code during software development. Unlike centralized version control systems, every developer's working copy of the code is also a repository that contains the full history of all changes. Git uses a staging area (the 'index') where commits can be formatted and reviewed before completing the commit. Branching in Git is lightweight — creating, merging, and deleting branches takes seconds because a branch is simply a movable pointer to a commit, not a full copy of the codebase.
